evals.prompt: I'm about to start working on a new feature for our Rails app — user notifications. I want to keep main clean. What should I do? I use fish shell.
evals.expected_output: Should check/install wt if not present (fish-specific install), then guide through `wt feature/user-notifications main` (or similar branch name), explain the worktree will be at ~/.wt/<project>/<branch>, mention .worktreekeep for .env files if relevant.

assertions.description: Response names the `wt` command as the solution, not just plain git
Includes the fish-specific install path or curl command for fish shell
Shows a concrete `wt <branch>` command to create the worktree
Doesn't fall back to raw `git worktree add` as the primary recommendation when wt is the tool

evals.prompt: I've been editing some files in my repo trying to figure out a bug, nothing committed yet. But now I need to drop everything and fix a critical production issue. I don't want to lose my debug changes.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `wt -w fix/critical-issue` (or similar), explaining that -w carries the unstaged changes to the new worktree safely, and they can return to the debug changes later via `wt switch`.

assertions.description: Surfaces the `-w` / `--with-changes` flag as the primary solution
Explains what -w does: stashes current changes and applies them in the new worktree
Mentions how to return to the debug changes (wt switch or the worktree path)

evals.prompt: My feature branch got merged into main yesterday. I have the worktree at ~/.wt/myapp/feature-payments. Also I notice I have like 5 other worktrees sitting around from old branches. How do I clean all this up?
evals.expected_output: Should guide through `wt remove -d feature-payments` for the merged branch, then `wt prune` for branches gone from remote, and `wt review` to audit all worktrees across projects with status indicators.

assertions.description: Mentions `wt remove -d` to delete worktree and branch together
Mentions `wt prune` for cleaning up gone remote branches
Mentions `wt review` for auditing all worktrees across projects
Explains what wt review shows: merged/closed, orphaned, has changes, etc.
